svg.text: implementation pointers → handler classes, inputs_schema, outputs_schema
⚠ Handler expects flat keys: repo_ref, feature_spec — NOT HERE
docker_worker_handler(state, node_config) — What it actually receives
✓ Handler reads these correctly from node_config
1. Data flow mismatch at subgraph boundary
current_commit = {objective, repo_url, repo_ref, constraints, title, acceptance_criteria, test_focus}
This enters the subgraph as state["current_commit"] — a nested dict.
docker_worker_handler expects flat keys: state["repo_ref"], state["feature_spec"]
Neither exists at the top level of subgraph state. The prompt gets no task details.
2. No typing — implicit key conventions everywhere
All state is dict[str, Any]. Key names are strings matched by convention between:
• decomposer output keys ↔ dispatcher input keys (global_context, commit_slices)
• state_mapping keys ↔ handler expected keys (repo_ref, feature_spec)
A typo anywhere is a silent runtime failure. No compile-time or IDE help.
3. Invented intermediary structures with no schema
"global_context" — archipelago-invented, sounds like a framework concept, no type
"current_commit" — dispatcher merges global_context + commit_slice, no type
"commit_slices" — list iterated via current_index, no type
docker_worker_handler was designed for flat state (old pipeline where config was injected into state).
The new decomposer → dispatcher → subgraph architecture produces nested structures that don't
match the handler's expectations. The handler needs to understand current_commit or the pipeline
needs to flatten the data before it reaches the handler.
